constraint_notes: Typically an institution or other item applying to a territorial jurisdiction; the head domain is broad, so these types are soft.
A person is affiliated with a political-party organization; no broader membership relation is treated as equivalent.

An organization, industry, facility, or process (typically ORG/LOC/MISC) points to a product, material, service, or other output (MISC). P176 is only a directional counterpart: it is limited to a product's main or final manufacturer, whereas P1056 also covers materials, services, and processes.
A person points to an employer; educational affiliation has a similar PER-to-ORG signature but a different role.
Founded entity points to founder; ownership and organizational leadership can share arguments but do not assert founding.
Player, team, or event points to a league or competition; sports-team membership is a distinct affiliation.
A territory or population points to a numeric unemployment rate; population typing is not uniquely determined.
A work points to its publisher; record label, production company, and original broadcaster are distinct dissemination or production roles.
Owned subjects span many types, so the head preference is empty; founder and operator roles do not imply ownership.

A geographic item (typically LOC) points to its administrative container (LOC). P150 is only a directional counterpart because it is limited to direct administrative subdivisions; P276 and P706 instead express non-administrative or physical-feature location.
Claimed territory points to the polity or administrative division making the claim; this is not ordinary containment or ownership.

A person, organization, group-like item, or creative work (PER/ORG/MISC) points to an event (MISC) in which it participated. P710 is only a directional counterpart because its participant domain and team guidance are not coextensive.
A creative work or artist points to a genre or artistic field; artist and genre coarse types remain broad.

A replacing person, organization, state, office, or item (typically PER/ORG/LOC/MISC) points to what it replaces of similar type. P1366 is its exact inverse; P155 is fixed-direction confusion because ordinary sequence following need not involve replacement.

A replaced person, organization, state, office, or item (typically PER/ORG/LOC/MISC) points to its replacement of similar type. P1365 is its exact inverse; P156 is fixed-direction confusion because ordinary continuation need not involve replacement.

Equipment, a facility, or a service (typically LOC/MISC) points to its operator (PER/ORG/MISC, including a profession or other entity). P127 is fixed-direction confusion because operation does not imply ownership; all types are soft.

A municipality (typically LOC) points to the administrative division (LOC) for which it is the governmental seat; P36 is the exact inverse in that normal scope. The project wording broadens the head to a settlement, so that extension still needs review.
A person, organization, or religious building points to a religion or worldview; ethnicity is a distinct identity attribute.
A person or people points to a language spoken, written, or signed; collective-people typing is ambiguous.

A fictional or fictionalized person, organization, place, or other entity (PER/ORG/LOC/MISC) points to its narrative work (MISC). P674 is only a directional counterpart because it covers the narrower character-in-work case.

An administrative territory (LOC) points to a direct administrative subdivision (LOC). P131 is only a directional counterpart because P131 broadly locates items within administrative territory and is not restricted to direct subdivisions.

A later series item points to its immediate predecessor, with both type preferences left open. P156 is its exact inverse; P1365 and P807 are fixed-direction confusions that respectively require replacement or separation.

An earlier series item points to its immediate successor, with both type preferences left open. P155 is its exact inverse; P1366 is fixed-direction confusion because replacement is stronger than sequence continuation.

An organization (ORG) points to its headquarters city or town (LOC); P740 and P937 are fixed-direction confusions for formation and work locations. The project phrase 'other place' is broader than the Wikidata city-or-town scope.

A production (typically MISC) points to an actor (PER). The Wikidata scope excludes voice-only roles and directs non-fiction participation elsewhere, while the project wording broadens this to performers and other productions.
Creative work points to its producer; director is a distinct production role with the same common argument signature.
A person, organization, or work points to an award or recognition it received.
A non-human subject points to its sovereign country; the broad subject domain makes a head-type preference unreliable.

A creative work or other object (typically MISC) points to its creator (PER/ORG) where no more specific property applies. P800 is only a directional counterpart because it reverses the narrower notable-work subset.
Taxon points to its closest parent taxon; general subclassing has a similar hierarchy shape but different semantics.
A person points to an explicitly established ethnic group; religion or worldview is a distinct identity attribute.
Role or musical work points to a performer; lyricist and composer may share people but assert different creative roles.

A product or physical item (MISC) points to its main or final manufacturer (PER/ORG). P1056 is only a directional counterpart because it also covers broader producers, materials, services, and processes; P178 is fixed-direction confusion for development.
Software, game, product, or work points to its developer; manufacturer is a distinct role, while generic creator may legitimately overlap.
An item points to the series containing it; generic part-of is broader and is not declared equivalent.
Person, animal, or fictional character points to birth location; death, citizenship, and residence share coarse types but differ semantically.
Two local administrative bodies have a reciprocal twinning partnership; swapping head and tail preserves meaning.
A political territory points to its legislative body; governmental-subject coarse typing may require review.
Person, animal, or fictional character points to death location; birth, citizenship, and residence share coarse types but differ semantically.
Body of water points to a country in its drainage or border basin; both arguments are geographic but roles are directional.
Geographic entity points to an adjacent or containing body of water; watercourse mouth and physical-feature location are distinct.

A child (PER) points to the child's father (PER). P40 is a directional counterpart, not an exact inverse, because the reversed child relation does not itself assert a male parent; P25 remains fixed-direction confusion for mother.

A person, military unit, organization, award, or office (typically PER/ORG/MISC) points to its armed-service branch (ORG). The project description omits awards and offices, so the broader Wikidata head scope is retained as soft typing.

A child (PER) points to the child's mother (PER). P40 is a directional counterpart, not an exact inverse, because the reversed child relation does not itself assert a female parent; P22 remains fixed-direction confusion for father.
Spouse relation is unchanged by argument exchange; sibling has the same symmetric PER-PER shape but a different kinship meaning.
Music recording points to a record-label brand; publisher and production company are distinct commercial roles.
Person points to country of citizenship; birth, death, and residence locations have the same coarse signature but different claims.

A film, audio, or performing-arts work (MISC) points to its production company (ORG); publisher, label, and broadcaster are fixed-direction confusions. The project wording additionally covers television, games, and other creative works, a broader scope needing review.

An object, structure, or event (typically LOC/MISC) points to its physical location (LOC). P131 is fixed-direction confusion for administrative containment; the type preferences are soft because the head domain is broad.
Narrower class points to broader class; instance-of and taxonomic parent relations have similar hierarchy shapes but different semantics.
Geographic entity points to its containing continent.
Any entity may point to its class, so the head preference is empty; subclass-of instead relates two classes.
Sibling relation is unchanged by argument exchange; spouse has the same symmetric PER-PER shape but a different kinship meaning.
Country or state points to its formal head of state; head of government is a distinct executive role.
A parent organization or unit (ORG) points to its child organization or unit (ORG). P749 is its exact inverse under the Wikidata organizational-parent meaning.

An administrative territory (LOC) points to its governmental-seat city or place (LOC). P1376 is the exact inverse in the normal municipality-seat scope; broader readings of settlement or place need review.
A component points to the whole of which it is part, with both coarse type preferences left open. P527 is its exact inverse across the same generic part-whole scope.
Film or performance work points to its original language; language is represented conservatively as MISC.
Country, region, organization, or other item points to an officially designated language; subject typing is broad.
Person points to a held office, role, or position; this is not generic organization membership.

A parent (PER) points to a child (PER). P22 and P25 are directional counterparts, not exact inverses, because they additionally assert that the reversed parent is respectively male or female; they are not fixed-direction confusions.
Work or software product points to a development or release platform.
Watercourse points to the body of water into which it drains; general adjacency to water is a different relation.

A radio or television program (MISC) points to its original network or service (typically ORG/MISC). P123 and P272 are fixed-direction confusions for publisher and production company; service need not be an organization.
Person or organization points to an organization, group, or body of which it is a member; specialized membership predicates remain distinct.
Organization or body points to its chairperson; organizational leadership does not imply founding.
Work, product, or item points to its country of origin; the broad head domain is represented as MISC.
Written work points to its author; screenplay and lyric writing are narrower roles with similar argument signatures.
A whole points to one of its components, with both coarse type preferences left open. P361 is its exact inverse across the same generic part-whole scope.
Athlete or player points to a sports team; league or competition participation is a distinct affiliation.
Person points to residence; birth, death, citizenship, and work locations share coarse types but assert different roles.
Person points to birth date or time; death date has the same PER-to-TIME signature but opposite lifecycle meaning.
Creative work points to director; screenwriter and producer are distinct production roles with similar arguments.
Person points to death date or time; birth date has the same PER-to-TIME signature but opposite lifecycle meaning.
Broad entity points to inception time; dissolution, publication, and generic start times are related temporal predicates but not equivalent.

An organization or building (typically ORG/LOC) points to the time it ceased to exist (TIME); inception and generic end time are fixed-direction confusions. The project phrase 'other entity' is broader than the Wikidata scope and needs review.
Work or product points to first publication or release time; inception and generic point-in-time predicates are distinct.
Work points to screenplay or script writer; generic author and director are distinct creative roles.
Broad entity, event, role, or statement points to start time; inception, end time, and point in time require distinct temporal readings.
Broad entity, event, role, or statement points to end time; dissolution, start time, and point in time are distinct temporal readings.
Event, entity, or statement points to a time at which it occurred or held; publication, start, and end predicates are more specific.
A governmental territory or body (typically LOC/ORG) points to its executive leader (PER). P35 is fixed-direction confusion for the distinct head-of-state role; these coarse types are soft.
A person or other participating item (typically PER/ORG/MISC) points to a military conflict (MISC). Generic event participation is broader and is not added as fixed-direction confusion.

A work (MISC) points to a character appearing in it (typically PER/MISC). P1441 is only a directional counterpart because its reversed scope also covers fictionalized places and other entities; the project's 'fictional character' wording is narrower than the source label.
Song or musical work points to lyricist; author, performer, and composer roles can share people but remain distinct.
Person points to an attended educational institution; employer has the same common type signature but a different affiliation.
Geographic entity points to a physical feature; administrative location and body-of-water adjacency are distinct, while generic location may overlap.

An event or process (MISC) points to an active participant (typically PER/ORG/MISC). P1344 is only a directional counterpart because its domain and P710's separate guidance for team participants make the scopes non-coextensive.

A person, idea, or other entity (typically PER/MISC) points to an influencing person, idea, or entity of similarly open type. The relation is directed, not symmetric, and both type lists are only soft preferences.
Group or organization points to formation location; headquarters and work locations are distinct organizational-place roles.

A child organization or unit (ORG) points to its parent organization or unit (ORG). P355 is its exact inverse under the Wikidata organizational-parent meaning; the project wording 'owns, contains, or controls' is broader and should not expand that equivalence.
A person or organization (PER/ORG) points to a notable work (MISC). P170 is only a directional counterpart because creator covers non-notable works and other created objects as well.

An entity founded or started by separation (typically ORG/LOC/MISC) points to its source entity of similarly broad type. P155 is fixed-direction confusion for ordinary series succession; the open Wikidata domain makes all listed types soft.
Narrative work points to its fictional or narrative setting; this is not an actual physical-location assertion.
Musical or audiovisual work points to composer; performer and lyricist are distinct musical roles.
Person or organization points to active work location; headquarters, residence, and formation locations are distinct roles.
